title: Writing: Who Are You and Where Are You From?
learningGoal: Construct full sentences in Hindi to introduce yourself and state your home country using the correct postpositions.
instructions: In this exercise, you will practice writing self-introductions. Remember that Hindi sentences end with the verb (SOV order). Use the word bank to translate the complex sentences. Pay special attention to where the word 'se' (from) is placed.
writingPrompt:
Translate the following self-introduction into Hindi:
1. Hello. How are you?
2. I am [Your Name]. (Use 'John' for the system check)
3. I am from America.
4. And who is this?
5. This is Maria. Maria is from Spain.
promptGoal: Building complex self-introductions using 'Main... hoon', 'se', and 'yeh... hai'.
referenceHints: Sentence 5: 'This Maria is. Maria Spain from is.'

modelExampleOutputs.targetText: नमस्ते। आप कैसे हैं? मैं जॉन हूँ। मैं अमेरिका से हूँ। और यह कौन है? यह मारिया है। मारिया स्पेन से है।
modelExampleOutputs.transliteration: Namaste. Aap kaise hain? Main John hoon. Main Amerika se hoon. Aur yeh kaun hai? Yeh Maria hai. Maria Spain se hai.
modelExampleOutputs.baseTranslation: Hello. How are you? I am John. I am from America. And who is this? This is Maria. Maria is from Spain.
modelExampleOutputs.annotation: Perfect SOV word order and correct use of verbs 'hoon' vs 'hai'.

evaluationCriteria.description: Places 'se' immediately after the country, and ends with 'hoon'.
Correctly uses 'yeh' to introduce someone else.

adminCorrectAnswerSet.sampleAnswer: नमस्ते। आप कैसे हैं? मैं जॉन हूँ। मैं अमेरिका से हूँ। और यह कौन है? यह मारिया है। मारिया स्पेन से है।
acceptableAlternates: Namaste. Aap kaise hain? Main John hoon. Main Amerika se hoon. Aur yeh kaun hai? Yeh Maria hai. Maria Spain se hai.
adminCorrectAnswerSet.grammarBaseline: Accepts transliteration. Strict checking of verb ending (SOV).
adminCorrectAnswerSet.lengthExpectation: A full paragraph of 6 short sentences.

criteria.name: Verb Placement
criteria.description: Verbs 'hain', 'hoon', and 'hai' must be at the very end of their respective clauses.

criteria.name: Postposition Placement
criteria.description: The word 'se' must immediately follow the noun it modifies (e.g., Amerika se).

feedbackRules.onPass: Masterful! You can now write full, complex introductions in Hindi.
feedbackRules.onFail: Please review the word order. In Hindi, it's 'I America from am'.
feedbackRules.onPartial: Good try! Make sure you don't mix up 'hoon' (am) and 'hai' (is). Maria takes 'hai', you take 'hoon'.

hintText: Read the 'Reference Hints' section closely to see the literal English translation of the Hindi word order.
commonMistakes: Writing 'Main hoon Amerika se' (English word order).
